records.ety: [L. temporalitas, in LL., possessions of the church: cf. F. temporalit.]

defs: The state or quality of being temporary; -- opposed to perpetuity.

That which pertains to temporal welfare; material interests; especially, the revenue of an ecclesiastic proceeding from lands, tenements, or lay fees, tithes, and the like; -- chiefly used in the plural.
quotes: Supreme head, . . . under God, of the spirituality and temporality of the same church.
